mnemonics: ETH - Every Transaction Happens (via smart contracts on a global computer)
GAS - Goes Away Swiftly (it's the fuel fee you pay for computation)
keyPoints: Ethereum is a decentralized global computer that executes smart contracts.
It uses a cryptocurrency called Ether (ETH) to pay for transaction fees and computational services, known as 'gas'.
Its transition from Proof-of-Work (PoW) to Proof-of-Stake (PoS) in 'The Merge' drastically reduced its energy consumption.
commonMistakes: Thinking gas fees are paid in Ethereum. (They are paid in Gwei, a denomination of ETH).
Believing smart contracts are stored on the blockchain in their high-level code. (They are compiled to bytecode before deployment).
